# datadog_incident_postmortem_template (Resource)

Provides a Datadog incident postmortem template resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog incident postmortem templates.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `incident_type` (String) The ID of the incident type this template is associated with. Immutable after creation.
- `name` (String) The name of the template.

### Optional

- `confluence_postmortem_settings` (Block, Optional) Settings for a postmortem template stored in Confluence. Required when location is confluence. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--confluence_postmortem_settings))
- `content` (String) The templated content of the postmortem, supporting Markdown and incident template variables.
- `google_docs_postmortem_settings` (Block, Optional) Settings for a postmortem template stored in Google Docs. Required when location is google_docs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--google_docs_postmortem_settings))
- `is_default` (Boolean) Whether this template is a default for its incident type. The API stores a timestamp; the effective default for an incident type is the template with the most recent default timestamp.
- `location` (String) The location where the postmortem is created and stored. Valid values are: datadog_notebooks, confluence, google_docs. Defaults to datadog_notebooks. The confluence and google_docs locations are gated behind their respective integrations and feature flags.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) Timestamp when the template was created.
- `id` (String) The ID of the postmortem template.
- `last_modified_by_user` (String) The ID of the user who last modified the template.
- `modified` (String) Timestamp when the template was last modified.

<a id="nestedblock--confluence_postmortem_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `confluence_postmortem_settings`

Optional:

- `account_id` (String) The ID of the Confluence integration account.
- `parent_id` (String) The ID of the parent Confluence page under which postmortems are created.
- `space_id` (String) The ID of the Confluence space where postmortems are created.


<a id="nestedblock--google_docs_postmortem_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `google_docs_postmortem_settings`

Optional:

- `account_id` (String) The ID of the Google Drive integration account.
- `parent_folder_id` (String) The ID of the Google Drive folder where postmortems are created.
